response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack web developer with strong software craftsmanship credentials, but the role requires deep hardware-level expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. The closest relevant experience is low-latency DLang services and brief C/C++ tooling work at National Instruments, which is far from performance architecture for deep-learning silicon. Years of experience meet the bar, but the domain mismatch is fundamental.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, and systems domains
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby using async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and teaching/mentoring track record
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon design feedback, or hardware performance-modeling experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning workload optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product full-stack engineering, not principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU performance background
